LAW AND MOTION 301, PETITIONER PETER HACKETT'S PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE ENFORCING SHAREHOLDER'S RIGHT TO INSPECT RECORDS OF CORP. WAS GRANTED. PETITIONER WILL HAVE ACCESS TO BOOKS AND RECORDS. THESE MUST BE MADE AVAILABLE WITHIN 14 DAYS EITHER AT THE COMPANY OR BOOKKEEPER'S BUSINESS. THE COURT WILL RETAIN JURISDICTION AND THE REQUESTS FOR ATTORNEY'S FEES AND AUDIT WERE DENIED. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR #8189

calendar.matters:
Notice Of Hearing On Petition For Writ Of Mandate Enforcing Shareholder'S Right To Inspext Records Of Corp
Off Calendar Dec-12-2006 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Ross B. Jones of Merrill Arnone & Jones, LLP, 707-528-2882, appearing for petitioner and moving party
Carter Ott of DLA Piper Rudnick Gray Cary, LLP, 836-2538, appearing for respondent and opposing party

Argued and PETITIONER PETER HACKETT'S Petition For Writ Of Mandate Enforcing Shareholder's Right To Inspect Records Of Corp. was granted. Petitioner will have access to books and records. These must be made available within 14 days either at the company or bookkeeper's business. The Court will retain jurisdiction and the requests for attorney's fees and audit were denied. Prevailing party to prepare form of order. (301/PJB/CK) Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR #8189
